title: The Cats Are Onto Something
summary_en: An exploratory essay that uses the seemingly irrational behavior of cats as a starting point to discuss deeper epistemological questions about how we form beliefs and models of the world. It argues that what appears as irrationality might sometimes be a rational response to an incomplete or misunderstood model.
summary_zh: 一篇探索性文章，以猫咪看似非理性的行为为起点，深入探讨了我们如何形成信念和世界模型的认知论问题。文章提出，表面上的非理性有时可能是在不完整或被误解的模型下做出的理性反应。
best_quote_en: The cat's 'mistake' is not in being irrational, but in operating from a model of reality that is simpler than ours—and in some contexts, more robust.
best_quote_zh: 猫咪的‘错误’并非在于非理性，而在于它运作的现实模型比我们的更简单——并且在某些情境下，更为稳健。
